name: large
description: Large benchmark project: four agents across three phases -- fan-out Q&A with parallel parks, contested cross-domain claims, and a genuine wait-cycle that must be detected and broken by the prop's cycle detector (not by message side effects). Ends with a SquireWatch quiescence check.

agents.name: qa
agents.description: Owns test plans and QA assets

steps.explanation: Phase 1: UI asks API for the dashboard endpoint contract.
steps.subject: Dashboard endpoint contract

steps.explanation: Phase 1: Data asks API which tables the server reads, creating fan-in load on one agent.
steps.subject: Tables read by server
steps.body: Which tables does the server query so the pipeline can populate them?

steps.explanation: UI parks on its question, one of two parallel parks against the same counterpart.
steps.resume_hint: Wire the dashboard once the contract arrives.

steps.explanation: Data parks on its question in parallel with UI's park.
steps.resume_hint: Build the pipeline once table names arrive.

steps.explanation: Tick one wakes API (two inbox items); tick two wakes UI and Data together (both parks satisfied, exercising the concurrency budget); tick three drains the answer messages.
Both parallel parks must be resumed after API answers.
Phase 2: QA must not be able to claim a UI-owned file without a grant.
Phase 2: QA requests a temporary grant to instrument the dashboard for testing.
steps.subject: Need dashboard.tsx for test hooks
steps.body: Please grant a temporary claim for src/ui/dashboard.tsx.

steps.explanation: QA parks until UI grants the contested claim.
steps.resume_hint: Add test hooks under the grant, then release.

steps.explanation: Wake UI to grant, then QA to claim under the grant, edit, and release.

Phase 3: seed message API->Data. The subject deliberately does NOT start with 'deadlock', so the stub's counterpart-broke-deadlock handler cannot clear the coming cycle as a side effect -- the prop's cycle detector must do it.
steps.subject: cycle seed one
steps.body: Seed message for the wait-cycle test.

steps.explanation: Mirror seed Data->API so each side has a message to park against.
steps.subject: cycle seed two
steps.body: Counterpart seed message for the wait-cycle test.

steps.explanation: Drain the seed messages first so the inbox-first rule cannot mask the cycle: after this tick both inboxes are empty.
API parks waiting on Data. No reply to the seed will ever come.
steps.resume_hint: Break the cycle with a documented assumption if designated.

steps.explanation: Data parks waiting on API, closing a genuine two-node wait-cycle with empty inboxes.
steps.resume_hint: Break the cycle with a documented assumption if designated.

steps.explanation: With no inbox work and no ready parks, the squire must detect the wait-cycle and wake the designated breaker (lexicographic minimum: api).
The counterpart wakes on the breaker's notification and clears its remaining parked task.
The cycle must be gone from the ledger after the breaker path runs.
QA expands into docs to write the test-coverage chapter, simulating a late domain shift.
steps.desc: Expands into documentation for the coverage chapter.

steps.explanation: QA broadcasts the shift so the domain map stays shared.
steps.subject: QA expanding into docs
steps.body: I have shifted domain to include docs/** for the coverage chapter.

steps.explanation: Run the squire as it runs in production until the swarm is quiescent: no running agents, empty inboxes, no parked tasks.
Nothing may remain parked at the end of the project.
The scenario must end with a clean ledger.
